title: Auto-load today's NYT Connections words via Cloudflare Worker proxy (OCR/manual as fallback)
description:
OCR fails ~10% of the time (100% on some puzzles), causing users to bounce. Market feedback: people prefer helper sites (connections-copilot.com, connectionscompanion.com) that already have the day's words loaded. Research confirmed NYT publishes the puzzle at https://www.nytimes.com/svc/connections/v2/{YYYY-MM-DD}.json (verified live: no auth, returns categories[].cards[].content = 16 words + positions). The endpoint sends NO Access-Control-Allow-Origin header, so a browser fetch is CORS-blocked — a same-origin server hop is required. This app is already on Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets, so we add our own Worker proxy (exactly what connections-copilot.com does).

Make 'today's 16 words already loaded' the DEFAULT, keeping OCR + manual entry as fallback. To honor NYT's tolerance line (helpers OK; recreating/archiving the game not OK): the Worker strips category titles/groupings SERVER-SIDE and returns only the 16 words in board-position (display, scrambled) order — the answer key never reaches the browser. No solve mode, current-window only (today + last ~7 days, NOT a browsable archive), one puzzle per request.
design: Primary path: SPA GET /api/puzzle[?date=YYYY-MM-DD] -> Cloudflare Worker (run_worker_first: [/api/*]) fetches NYT v2 server-side, flattens categories[].cards by position, DISCARDS titles/groups, returns {date, words[16]} with short edge cache. Client maps through normalizeTileText -> existing loadPuzzle(words). Date window enforced server-side (today ET .. today-6, >= 2023-06-12 launch). Shared fetch+transform module reused by a Vite dev middleware so npm run dev works. Fallback chain: daily fetch -> OCR -> manual. UX: auto-load today when no saved puzzle (loading screen); resume + 'Today's Puzzle' menu card when a puzzle exists; recent-day chips; non-affiliation disclaimer + official-game link.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/puzzle returns {date, words:[16 uppercase strings]} and NEVER includes category titles/groupings; words are in board-position order. Date param validated to today..today-6 ET (and >= 2023-06-12); out-of-range/future -> error JSON, client falls back. No saved puzzle -> today auto-loads onto the board; saved puzzle -> resume + button to load today. Fetch failure/timeout -> graceful fall to OCR/manual, never a blank board or hard error. npm run dev serves /api/puzzle locally. npm run lint and npm run build pass.
notes: Implemented + verified on branch connections-r4m; PR #1 (https://github.com/bigintersmind/connections-sorter/pull/1). Worker proxy at /api/puzzle returns words-only (groupings stripped), date window today..today-6 ET enforced server-side, OCR/manual fallback intact. Close on merge.

title: Homepage menu revamp: explainer + preview + demo puzzle button
description: Replace the misplaced 'Tap two tiles to swap' hint on the menu with a real landing surface: tagline, 4x4 board preview graphic, third 'Try Demo Puzzle' button (hand-crafted sample), and 'How it works' 3-step section. Also tighten SEO meta/noscript copy and add semantic landmarks (main/nav/section, h1/h2). Lowers barrier to entry for new users and improves search-engine readability. Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/the-homepage-says-tap-fizzy-whale.md

title: Favicon: replace generic lightning glyph with Connections 4x4 grid
description: Current /public/favicon.svg is a generic purple lightning bolt with no connection to the site's theme or purpose. The OG image (public/og-image.png) already establishes the visual identity: a 4x4 grid of rounded tiles in the canonical Connections colors (yellow #f9df6d, green #a0c35a, blue #b0c4ef, purple #ba81c5), in solved order top→bottom. Make the favicon match — same grid, same colors, same order — so the tab icon instantly reads as 'Connections' and reinforces the brand established by the OG card.
close_reason: Replaced generic purple lightning SVG with a 4x4 grid in the canonical Connections colors (yellow, green, blue, purple, solved order top→bottom), matching the visual identity already established by public/og-image.png. Lint passes; renders cleanly at both 256px and 32px.

title: SEO improvements: prerender body, social card, robots/sitemap, canonical, JSON-LD, title tweak
description: Improve discoverability and link previews. Six changes: (1) Static H1+intro in #root so empty SPA shell stops hiding content from crawlers and social previews. (2) Add og:image + twitter:card=summary_large_image with a 1200x630 preview. (3) public/robots.txt + public/sitemap.xml so indexability is explicit. (4) <link rel=canonical> to lock the primary URL. (5) WebApplication JSON-LD in <head>. (6) Title includes 'NYT Connections' so the real search query has something to match.

title: Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut on upload screen
description:
## Parent

connections-ynq

## What to build

A keyboard shortcut on the upload screen so desktop users can press Cmd/Ctrl+V to load a clipboard image into the preview without clicking the "Paste from clipboard" button. Reuses the clipboard image extractor from connections-ren; the listener routes paste-event clipboard data through the same downstream wiring (`pickFile()` on success, `setError()` on failure with the same three error messages).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] While the upload screen is mounted on a desktop browser (Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge) with an image on the clipboard, pressing Cmd+V (macOS) or Ctrl+V (Windows/Linux) renders that image in the preview area
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut works both when no image has been loaded yet and when a preview is already showing (in which case it replaces the current preview)
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut is a no-op while OCR is running (the `running` flag is true)
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image, the error message reads: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, the error message reads: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- [ ] When the paste event handler throws or otherwise errors, the error message reads: `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`
- [ ] The paste event listener is added when the upload screen mounts and removed when it unmounts
- [ ] On screens other than the upload screen, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V has no behavior change related to image paste
- [ ] The button-click flow from connections-ren continues to work identically
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

## Blocked by

connections-ren (Paste-from-clipboard button on upload screen) — this slice imports the clipboard image extractor introduced in that ticket.
close_reason: Code complete in bc401ec. Cross-browser desktop verification gate is on the parent PRD (connections-ynq) and a human, per AFK contract.

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Add a Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut on the upload screen that loads a clipboard image into the preview, reusing the extractor from connections-ren.

**Current behavior:**
After connections-ren ships, users on the upload screen can load a clipboard image by clicking the new "Paste from clipboard" button. Pressing Cmd+V (macOS) or Ctrl+V (Windows/Linux) on the upload screen has no effect; desktop users must click the button.

**Desired behavior:**
While the upload screen is mounted, pressing Cmd+V or Ctrl+V loads any image on the clipboard into the upload screen's preview area, with the same downstream behavior as clicking the paste button. The shortcut works in both states: when no image has been loaded yet, and when a preview is already showing — in the latter case it replaces the current preview (this is non-destructive because OCR has not yet run). The shortcut is a no-op while OCR is in flight. The listener is added when the upload screen mounts and removed when it unmounts; pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V on other screens has no related effect.

**Key interfaces:**

- **Clipboard image extractor** — Already shipped in connections-ren. The paste-event listener takes `clipboardData.items` from the `ClipboardEvent`, passes them through this extractor, and routes the resulting discriminated union to the same downstream calls used by the button click (preview-loading helper on `ok`, error-message state on `no-image` / `unsupported-format` / `error`).

- **Upload screen component** — The existing React component (post-connections-ren). The `paste` event listener is added via a `useEffect` whose lifetime matches the component. The listener checks the in-flight OCR flag (already present in component state) and is a no-op while OCR is running.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] While the upload screen is mounted on a desktop browser (Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge) with an image on the clipboard, pressing Cmd+V or Ctrl+V renders that image in the preview area
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut works both when no image has been loaded yet and when a preview is already showing — in the latter case the new image replaces the existing preview
- [ ] The keyboard shortcut is a no-op while OCR is running
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V surfaces the error message exactly: `No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first.`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V surfaces exactly: `That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker.`
- [ ] When the paste-event handler errors (or the extractor returns `{ kind: "error" }`), the message is exactly: `Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead.`
- [ ] The paste event listener is added when the upload screen mounts and removed when it unmounts (no listener leaks across screens)
- [ ] On screens other than the upload screen, pressing Cmd/Ctrl+V has no behavior change related to image paste
- [ ] The button-click flow from connections-ren continues to work identically
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

**Out of scope:**

- The button-click entry path — already shipped in connections-ren
- Reimplementing the extractor — reuse the one introduced in connections-ren
- Tests
- Mobile-specific paste behavior — iOS Safari fires `paste` events only inside editable elements, so this listener will be a desktop-only path in practice; this is expected and documented in the parent PRD

**Verification gate before merge:**
The parent PRD requires manual cross-browser verification on desktop Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge before close-out. The agent's responsibility ends at "code complete, `npm run lint` passing, PR opened". A human performs the cross-browser check before merge.

**Acceptance criteria satisfied (code complete):**

- [x] Works in both empty and preview states (preview is replaced — non-destructive because OCR has not run)
- [x] No-op while `running` is true
- [x] `no-image` → `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- [x] `unsupported-format` → `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`

- [x] Listener attached on mount via `useEffect` and removed on unmount — does not leak across screens
- [x] Button-click flow from connections-ren is unchanged (only `pickFile` was refactored to `useCallback` with empty deps; behavior identical)
- [x] `npm run lint` passes; `npm run build` passes

**Pending human verification (per parent PRD `connections-ynq`):**
- Cross-browser desktop check on Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge with an actual screenshot on the clipboard before merge.

title: Paste-from-clipboard button on upload screen
description:
## Parent

connections-ynq

## What to build

A new "Paste from clipboard" peer button on the upload screen, alongside the existing "Choose an image" button. When clicked, it reads any image on the user's clipboard via the Async Clipboard API, loads it into the existing preview, and routes through the existing `pickFile()` and OCR flow. Includes the underlying clipboard image extractor module that decides what's an acceptable image and what error to surface.

When the browser doesn't support clipboard image reads, the button is hidden entirely — no visual change to users on those browsers.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] On the upload screen, a "Paste from clipboard" button is visible when `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` is true
- [ ] On browsers where that capability check is false, the paste button is not rendered, and the screen falls back to the existing single-button layout
- [ ] The paste button uses the same dropzone-card visual style as "Choose an image": clipboard icon (📋, 32px), label "Paste from clipboard", description "From a screenshot you copied"
- [ ] The paste button is stacked vertically below the existing "Choose an image" button
- [ ] On iOS Safari 16.4+ with a screenshot in the clipboard, clicking the paste button renders the image in the preview area
- [ ] On desktop Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge with an image in the clipboard, clicking the paste button renders the image in the preview area
- [ ] After a successful paste, clicking "Extract Words" runs OCR and produces the same output as uploading the same image via the file picker
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image (empty, or text only), the error message reads: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, the error message reads: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- [ ] When `navigator.clipboard.read()` throws (permission denied, etc.), the error message reads: `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`
- [ ] The clipboard image extractor is implemented as a pure function returning a discriminated union with kinds `ok`, `no-image`, `unsupported-format`, `error` — accepting `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`; rejecting `image/heic`, `image/heif`, `image/svg+xml` with `unsupported-format`
- [ ] The existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

## Blocked by

None – can start immediately.
close_reason: Implemented in ff057f8 — paste button + extractor module land green on lint and build. iOS hardware verification remains the human merge gate per the parent PRD.

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

## Agent Brief

**Category:** enhancement
**Summary:** Add a "Paste from clipboard" peer button on the upload screen, with the underlying clipboard image extractor module.

**Current behavior:**
The upload screen offers a single entry point for loading a screenshot: a file picker. Users on iOS who use the screenshot toolbar's "Copy" button (introduced in iOS 16+) cannot paste their screenshot directly — they have to save it to Photos first or abandon the screenshot path. The upload screen renders a single dropzone-style button labeled "Choose an image", feeds the chosen `File` into an internal helper that creates an object URL and shows a preview, and runs OCR via Tesseract.js when the user clicks "Extract Words".

**Desired behavior:**
A second peer button labeled "Paste from clipboard" is rendered on the upload screen, stacked vertically below "Choose an image" and matching its dropzone-card visual style. Clicking the paste button calls `navigator.clipboard.read()`, finds the first acceptable image on the clipboard, and feeds the resulting `Blob` into the same downstream preview/OCR flow used by the file picker. The paste button is rendered only when `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` are both truthy; otherwise it is omitted entirely (the layout falls back to the current single-button look).

The MIME-type policy and clipboard-data interpretation live in a new pure module — the **clipboard image extractor** — that returns a discriminated union, separating the "what's on the clipboard" question from the "what does the React component do about it" question.

**Key interfaces:**

- **Clipboard image extractor** — A pure function that takes either an array of `ClipboardItem` (from `navigator.clipboard.read()`) or an array of `DataTransferItem` (from a `paste` event's `clipboardData.items`) and returns a discriminated union with shape:
  - `{ kind: "ok", blob: Blob }` — first accepted image found
  - `{ kind: "no-image" }` — no entries with image MIME types
  - `{ kind: "unsupported-format" }` — only HEIC / HEIF / SVG image entries present
  - `{ kind: "error" }` — anything else, including thrown errors from `getType()` / `getAsFile()`

  Accepted MIME types: `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`. Rejected with `unsupported-format`: `image/heic`, `image/heif`, `image/svg+xml`. When multiple images are present, the first accepted item wins.

- **Clipboard capability helper** — A pure function returning `window.isSecureContext && !!navigator.clipboard?.read`. Used to gate rendering of the paste button.

- **Upload screen component** — The existing React component handling screenshot upload and OCR. It already exposes (internally) a function that takes an image `File`/`Blob`, validates the MIME prefix, sets preview state, and creates an object URL — the paste flow reuses this entry point unchanged. It also already exposes an error-message state rendered as a one-line message below the action buttons — the paste flow reuses this for failure messages.

**Acceptance criteria:**

- [ ] On the upload screen, a "Paste from clipboard" button is visible when the clipboard capability check returns true
- [ ] When the capability check returns false, the paste button is not rendered, and the screen falls back to the existing single-button layout
- [ ] The paste button uses the same dropzone-card visual style as "Choose an image": clipboard icon (📋, 32px), label "Paste from clipboard", description "From a screenshot you copied"
- [ ] The paste button is stacked vertically below "Choose an image"
- [ ] Clicking the paste button with an image on the clipboard renders that image in the upload screen's preview area, indistinguishable from selecting the same image via the file picker
- [ ] After a successful paste, clicking "Extract Words" runs OCR and produces the same output that uploading the same image via the file picker would produce
- [ ] When the clipboard contains no image, the error message reads exactly: `No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first.`
- [ ] When the clipboard contains only HEIC/HEIF/SVG, the error message reads exactly: `That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker.`
- [ ] When `navigator.clipboard.read()` throws (permission denied, etc.) or the extractor returns `{ kind: "error" }`, the error message reads exactly: `Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead.`
- [ ] The clipboard image extractor returns the four-kind discriminated union described above for both `ClipboardItem[]` and `DataTransferItem[]` input shapes
- [ ] The existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged (same clicks, same behavior, same OCR output on the same input)
- [ ] `npm run lint` passes

**Out of scope:**

- The Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut entry path — tracked separately as connections-ugt, which depends on this ticket
- Tests for the extractor or any other module — the project has no test runner configured today; per the parent PRD, no test runner is being introduced as part of this work
- Telemetry or analytics on which entry path users prefer
- HEIC decode support via a third-party library
- Modifying the OCR pipeline (Tesseract setup, MIME whitelist, post-processing)
- A separate "Paste a different image" button in the preview state

**Verification gate before merge:**
The parent PRD requires manual verification on real iOS Safari 16.4+ hardware before close-out. The agent's responsibility ends at "code complete, `npm run lint` passing, PR opened". A human performs the iOS hardware check before merge.

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
> *This was generated by AI during AFK execution.*

Implemented in ff057f8.

Acceptance criteria status:

- [x] Paste button visible when `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` is true (gated via `hasClipboardReadSupport()` lazy-init in `UploadScreen`)
- [x] Paste button not rendered when capability check is false; layout falls back to single-button look (the wrapper div renders just the file-picker button)
- [x] Same dropzone-card visual style — clipboard icon (📋, 32px), label "Paste from clipboard", description "From a screenshot you copied"
- [x] Stacked vertically below "Choose an image" via the new `dropzoneStack` wrapper (gap 12, marginTop 16 moved off `dropzone` onto the wrapper to avoid double-spacing)
- [x] Click handler calls `navigator.clipboard.read()` → `extractClipboardImage(items)` → `pickFile(blob)` on `ok`, `setError(...)` on the other kinds. Re-uses the existing `pickFile()` and `setError()` paths so the OCR pipeline downstream is unchanged.
- [x] Error message — no image: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- [x] Error message — only HEIC/HEIF/SVG: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- [x] Error message — `read()` throws or extractor returns `error`: `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`
- [x] Extractor (`src/clipboardImage.js`) is a pure async function returning the four-kind discriminated union, accepting both `ClipboardItem[]` and `DataTransferItem[]`. MIME policy: accept `image/png`/`image/jpeg`/`image/webp`; reject `image/heic`/`image/heif`/`image/svg+xml` with `unsupported-format`. First accepted item wins. Errors during `getType()`/`getAsFile()` take precedence over `unsupported-format` so a partial-failure clipboard surfaces the actionable error message rather than a misleading "format not supported".
- [x] File-picker upload flow unchanged (same input ref, same `pickFile()`, same `runOcr()`)
- [x] `npm run lint` passes
- [x] `npm run build` passes (209 KB main + 17 KB lazy chunk, in line with prior builds)

Adjacent fix bundled in: added `public/tesseract` to `eslint.config.js` `globalIgnores`. The Tesseract WASM/worker mirror is generated by `predev`/`prebuild`, is already in `.gitignore`, and shouldn't be linted as project source. Without this, the lint AC isn't verifiable locally after any `npm run dev` or `npm run build`.

Verification gate per the parent PRD: real iOS Safari 16.4+ hardware check of the paste path is still pending and is the human-side merge gate. Browser/UI verification on desktop wasn't run as part of this iteration — happy to wire up a Playwright check in a follow-up if useful, but the parent PRD explicitly declined a test runner so I left it alone.

Sibling slice connections-ugt (Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut) is unblocked — the extractor's `DataTransferItem[]` path is already in place for that listener to call.

title: PRD: Paste screenshot from clipboard on upload screen
description:
## Problem Statement

On iOS 16+, users can save a screenshot directly to the clipboard from the screenshot toolbar instead of saving it to Photos. For users who use this flow as their default, the Connections puzzle app currently has no way to accept a clipboard image — the only entry point is the file picker, which routes through Photos. These users have to save the screenshot to Photos first (an extra step) or abandon the screenshot path entirely.

Desktop users hit the same gap from the other direction: they expect Cmd/Ctrl+V to "just work" when an image is on the clipboard, but the upload screen ignores paste events.

## Solution

Add a "Paste from clipboard" affordance to the upload screen as a first-class peer to the existing "Choose an image" button. On iOS, the affordance is a tap target. On desktop, the same Blob can also arrive via Cmd/Ctrl+V. Either way, the pasted image flows into the existing OCR pipeline — same preview, same "Extract Words" confirmation, same output.

## User Stories

1. As an iOS user who copied a Connections screenshot using the screenshot toolbar's "Copy" button, I want to paste it directly into the upload screen, so that I don't have to save to Photos first.
2. As a desktop user with a screenshot on my clipboard, I want to press Cmd/Ctrl+V on the upload screen and have my screenshot loaded, so that the interaction matches my muscle memory from every other web app.
3. As a user on a browser that doesn't support clipboard image reads, I want the upload screen to look unchanged, so that I'm not shown a feature I can't use.
4. As a user whose clipboard has the wrong contents (empty, text-only, or an unsupported format), I want a clear and specific error message, so that I know what to do next instead of seeing a generic OCR failure later.
5. As a user who pasted the wrong image, I want to paste a different one without having to navigate away first, so that recovering from a clipboard mistake is fast.

## Implementation Decisions

### Module decomposition

- **Clipboard image extractor** — A pure function that accepts heterogeneous clipboard data (either the `ClipboardItem[]` returned by `navigator.clipboard.read()` or the `DataTransferItem[]` from a `paste` event's `clipboardData.items`) and returns a discriminated union with one of four kinds: `ok` (carries the Blob), `no-image` (no image-typed entries at all), `unsupported-format` (only HEIC/HEIF/SVG present), or `error` (anything else). Encapsulates the MIME type policy, the unification of the two API shapes, and the precedence rule (first accepted item wins when multiple images are present).
- **Clipboard capability check** — A pure helper returning whether `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` are both truthy. Used to gate rendering of the paste button.
- **UploadScreen integration** — Modifications to the existing upload component: conditional render of the paste button, click handler that calls `navigator.clipboard.read()` and routes the result through the extractor, a paste-event listener attached via `useEffect` while the component is mounted, and downstream wiring into the existing `pickFile()` and `setError()` paths. The OCR pipeline itself is unchanged.

### MIME type policy

- Accepted: `image/png`, `image/jpeg`, `image/webp`.
- Explicitly rejected with a specific error: `image/heic`, `image/heif`, `image/svg+xml`.
- Anything else: treated as no-image-found.

### UI

- The paste button is a peer to "Choose an image", stacked vertically below it, using the same dropzone-card visual style: clipboard icon (📋, 32px), label "Paste from clipboard", description "From a screenshot you copied".
- When the capability check returns false, the paste button is hidden entirely. The layout collapses to the existing single-button look — no visual regression in unsupported environments.

### Two entry paths, one downstream behavior

- Button click: `navigator.clipboard.read()` → extractor → either `pickFile(blob)` or `setError(message)`.
- Cmd/Ctrl+V: `paste` event listener inspects `event.clipboardData.items` → extractor → same downstream wiring. Listener is mounted while the upload screen is mounted. No-op while OCR is running. Works in both the empty and preview states (re-pasting replaces the current preview, which is non-destructive because OCR hasn't run yet).

### Error messages (reuse existing `setError` UI)

- `no-image` from extractor: `"No image on your clipboard. Copy a screenshot first."`
- `unsupported-format` from extractor: `"That image format isn't supported. Copy a screenshot, or use the file picker."`
- `error` from extractor, plus any thrown exception from `clipboard.read()` (permission denied, unknown errors): `"Couldn't paste from clipboard. You can choose a file instead."`

### Flow consistency

- After a successful paste, the preview renders and the user must click "Extract Words" — identical to the file-picker flow. No auto-OCR. The clipboard might hold a stale screenshot from earlier; the preview is the safety net.

## Testing Decisions

No tests are being added as part of this work. The project has no test runner configured today, and the MIME policy is short enough to eyeball-verify in code review. A real-hardware acceptance check on iOS Safari (see acceptance criteria below) covers the highest-stakes failure mode.

## Acceptance criteria

- Paste button visible on the upload screen when `window.isSecureContext && navigator.clipboard?.read` is true; hidden otherwise.
- On iOS Safari 16.4+ with a screenshot in the clipboard: clicking the paste button loads the image into the preview, and clicking "Extract Words" produces the same OCR output as the file-picker flow on the same image.
- On desktop Chrome / Safari / Firefox / Edge: both the button click and Cmd/Ctrl+V load the clipboard image into the preview.
- Empty clipboard, text-only clipboard, HEIC-only clipboard, and permission-denied each surface the matching error message.
- Existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged.
- Verified on real iOS hardware before close-out — particularly that `image/png` is what arrives from the screenshot toolbar's "Copy", and that the HEIC error path actually triggers (or that iOS Safari converts to PNG transparently and HEIC never appears).

## Out of Scope

- Telemetry or analytics on which entry path users prefer. The app has no telemetry today; introducing it is its own decision.
- A separate "Paste a different image" button in the preview state. Cmd/Ctrl+V covers re-entry; a button for an action almost no user will take twice in a row would add visual weight without payoff.
- HEIC support via a third-party decoder. iOS Safari typically gives PNG from the screenshot toolbar, so HEIC is a corner case (Photos copy, not screenshot copy). A specific error message is sufficient.
- Setting up a test runner. Considered and explicitly declined — see Testing Decisions.

## Further Notes

- iOS Safari only fires `paste` events inside editable elements, so the keyboard listener is effectively a no-op there. iOS users go through the button. The listener is a desktop-only path in practice.
- Once iOS records a "Don't Allow" response to its Allow Paste dialog, subsequent reads in the same session fail with `NotAllowedError`. The generic error message gives users a path forward without trying to re-prompt.
close_reason: Both implementation slices (connections-ren button, connections-ugt Cmd/Ctrl+V) shipped and merged. Follow-up review hardening landed in e1e8919 (target check on window paste listener, devtools logging at the four catch sites, comment rewrite). Manual hardware verification per acceptance criteria remains the user's call but the code is in main.

comments.author: Ivan
comments.text:
Broken into vertical slices via /issues:

- **connections-ren** — Paste-from-clipboard button on upload screen (no blockers; covers user stories 1, 3, 4, and the button half of 5)
- **connections-ugt** — Cmd/Ctrl+V keyboard shortcut on upload screen (blocked by connections-ren; covers user story 2 and completes 5)

Both classified AFK at planning time with the understanding that the agent stops at "code complete + lint passing"; manual hardware/cross-browser verification still required before merge per the PRD's acceptance criteria. Final HITL/AFK assignment happens at /triage.

title: Paste screenshot from clipboard on upload screen
description: On iOS 16+, users can save a screenshot directly to the clipboard instead of Photos, but the app currently has no way to accept that — the only entry point is the file picker. Add a 'Paste from clipboard' affordance on the upload screen so a clipboard image can be fed into the existing OCR pipeline. Desktop users (Cmd/Ctrl+V) benefit too.
design:
Add a 'Paste from clipboard' button alongside the existing 'Choose an image' button on UploadScreen (src/App.jsx:546). On click, call navigator.clipboard.read(), iterate ClipboardItems, find one whose types include 'image/png' or 'image/jpeg', call getType() to get a Blob, and pass it to the existing pickFile() at src/App.jsx:469 — no changes to the OCR pipeline needed since pickFile already handles any image Blob/File.

Feature-detect navigator.clipboard?.read and hide the button if unsupported (older browsers, non-HTTPS contexts).

Reuse setError for the failure modes: permission denied, no image on clipboard, unsupported MIME (e.g. HEIC if Tesseract can't read it — verify before shipping).

Optional polish: also attach a 'paste' event listener on the upload screen container so desktop users can Cmd/Ctrl+V without clicking the button. iOS won't fire this reliably outside editable elements, so the button is the load-bearing path.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'Paste from clipboard' button visible on upload screen when navigator.clipboard.read is available
- Clicking it on iOS Safari 16.4+ with a screenshot in the clipboard loads the image into the preview and runs OCR identically to file upload
- Clicking it on desktop Chrome/Safari/Firefox/Edge does the same
- Permission denied, empty clipboard, and non-image clipboard contents all surface a clear error via the existing error UI
- Button is hidden (not just disabled) on browsers without clipboard image read support
- Existing file-picker upload flow is unchanged
notes: Context: iOS 16+ added 'Copy' alongside 'Save to Photos' in the screenshot toolbar, and many users now use it as their default. The Async Clipboard API requires HTTPS (we have it via Cloudflare Workers) and a user gesture (the button click satisfies this). iOS shows a one-time 'Allow Paste' permission prompt — that's OS behavior, not something to engineer around.
